Good price and better than hooks, but has issues with the plating I ordered a pack of these to support dangle earrings to sell at a craft show. Most of them were completely plated, but a number of them had spots on the part that goes through the ear where the plating had been scratched/disrupted in some way. The opening/closing was also less than a smooth motion, but not really an issue for the price.

It is, however, difficult to find lever backs without some form of orn... Review 4: rating 3.0. Not too good quality product but for the price it's fair For the price the seller offers for the product, as leverbacks are usually a lot more expensive than regular fish hook earrings. I appreciate the price, however, the silver coating is not shiny as I'd like for my jewelry to be, and even after wiping to clean the surface, it's not quite shiny, and the silver coating is not all the same quality. Even though the price is good, I will spend more for other leverbacks with even silve... Review 5: rating 5.0. Great looking leverback I found the leverback earring very good looking, works very well, strong and easy to use. I will order many more. Bright and shiny I believe you can use as an enhancer. Review 6: rating 4.0. Good product for the price Very cheaply made, but you get what you pay for and you can't beat this price. Some of them have issues with not being able to move the hinge smoothly, but they still work. I have made dozens of earrings with these and so far I have had just ONE break, so they are holding up fine. Review 7: rating 3.0. Okay, but don't last forever Out of the package, these are nice, shiny leverbacks. And for the price I can't really complain. However, a large number of them are very stiff when you go to open and close them--some are so stuck that I had to pitch them. Also, the hinge mechanism seems rather cheap in its alignment. When you go to reclose the back, it will often not meet up smoothly with the rest of the earring. I also find that these are noticeably tarnished after a couple weeks. Jewe... Review 8: rating 1.0. JUNK! DO NOT BUY!!! Ok...so I never give bad reviews, however these are total CRAP!. First of all if I would have realized they shipped from China I would not have ordered them because they took forever to get. Second, they DON'T OPEN WITHOUT USING NEEDLE NOSE PLIERS!!!. I guess the saying you get what you pay for is in order here. Review 9: rating 2.0. Where ARE they? I would rate these if they would arrive. I really need lever back earrings for many of my designs, so I'm getting a bit frustrated. Review 10: rating 5.0. Great leverbacks! I've gotten into making jewelry recently and these are great for making earrings. The leverback style is my favorite and these worked out nicely on my projects Review 11: rating 1.0. Cornerback earrings I received the wrong earrings, I ordered two different kinds of wires and received three of the same wires with silver balls and springs.very dissatisfied.never received the leverback earrings. Review 12: rating 5.0. No more lost earrings So easy to switch from the plain hook type of earring to one that will stay put when you put it on. Review 13: rating 1.0. I have purchased many times from this vendor, but ... I have purchased many times from this vendor, but these were not silver plated. They were more of a gray & would not shine up to silver. Absolutely unacceptable! I have missed so many sales due to the last 3 orders (1 order never was delivered). I was told it was stuck in customs. Review 14: rating 1.0. Not as advertised The description states these are silver plated, but what arrived appears to just be stainless steel. Considering the price it's not worth the trouble of sent them all the way back to China. Review 15: rating 5.0. Nice quality for the price I wasn't sure what to expect with these being so cheap - I expected a "you get what you pay for" product. I was pleasantly surprised! The clasps are sturdy and feel much better than the usual French hooks my daughter has had to use with the earrings she makes. They're nice and light so they don't pull my holes in my ears. Review 16: rating 2.0. Metal Snaps When I went to attach the decorative part of the earring the metal snapped on about 20% of the lever backs. That makes me wonder about the long term structural integrity of the ones that didn't snap immediately. Will they snap later and cause my customer to be dissatisfied with my product? Review 17: rating 5.0. Lever back earrings I picked this item because it was priced right for me, I thought it was a good deal. I think all crafters who make earrings should get these. The quaility is good for your customers. I plan to make a lot of earrings since I got the lever backs and they stay in your ear better than the others, it's like they lock in your ear. Review 18: rating 3.0. Faulty earring pieces They're alright, many pieces don't open quite right and then won't align properly when closing. Hopefully for my needs I can get use out of some, the price was fair, but I may be too ashamed to use them in simple gifts. Review 19: rating 1.0. leverback ear wires I am very unhappy with the product. It says they are silver plated, but I don't think so. The wires are very dull gray. I cannot use this in my jewelry. I will not buy from this seller again Review 20: rating 1.0. These are very small! I have medium ear lobes and they actually cup my ear lobes. This makes them very difficult to put on. The curved part, instead of coming out the back of the lobe, starts threading down into the lobe itself. They need to be twice this size!

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Super easy! First time using resin—very easy n turned out great! Only mixed both in 1 cup n then poured from there n had no issues. So excited !

Review 2: rating 5.0. Great quality [[VIDEOID:726681b7502c95eef11f11eecaf41d5e]] I made 5 letters out of two table spoons of the resin and mixed it with ultra sparkle glitter and a little bit of foil.  All the letters came out great and I have more than half the bottle left over.  I had no bubbles and it dried quickly and smoothly.  This was my first time using it so this kit is definitely beginner friendly and I will be buying more for other projects.  I used a blow dryer in between layers when pouring a...

Review 3: rating 5.0. Excellent product The product was easy to dispense into cups for mixing. The bottles / tips make the process very easy & smooth. The resin mixes easily with minimal bubbles. It flows easily into the mold and settles quickly. Most small bubbles floated to the surface & popped. The tip about using a butane torch to bring all bubbles to the surface worked like a champ. I will definitely buy more of this resin.

Review 4: rating 5.0. Great product and  received item in 2 days This is my first time using resin and it was a great experience the product has no odor and is very easy to work with.

Review 5: rating 1.0. Wouldn’t recommend to people with asthma. It’s easy to use but it wasn’t safe for me because I have asthma and it was bad to inhale.

Review 6: rating 5.0. Yes I didnt pay enough attention to the size before ordering, so I was disappointed with that. However, it's a good product!

Review 7: rating 2.0. 4 days to cure and material is still pliable  isn't acrylic supposed to HARD? followed directions CAREFULLY allowed extra days to cure.  Acrylic does not get "hard" but remains somewhat pliable.  Feels hard to touch, but under a little pressure it deforms!

My first attempt came out like rubber, but that was my fault since I didn't properly measure the amount of resin.

Every time I turn around, my son is "borrowing" my 40 year old Fiskars shears (which were still nice and sharp) for whatever terribly important things college guys do with scissors. I decided that the thing to do was leave my much loved shears for my son to use, and purchased 2 pairs of these based upon the fact that they were Singer brand and they were pink. Glad I bought a backup from a different brand to continue making mats for the animals cages at the Humane Society shelter- Review 3: rating 1.0. Didn't work These scissors did not cut through any of the thicker fabrics I have. I kept them for regular use, but beware you get what you pay for. Review 4: rating 2.0. Poor sissor quality Does not cut fabric very good and are not sharp Review 5: rating 3.0. The scissors are comfortable, but they just don't cut as cleanly as ... I'm not really impressed. I'll stick to Fiskars from now on. My old fiskars cut better than these scissors. Review 6: rating 1.0. DO NOT BUY Can't cut through a t-shirt. DO NOT BUY! Review 7: rating 1.0. If all you will be cutting is ribbon or paper, you might be happy with these but for fabric, I suggest investing $20 in a pair of real fabric scissors. Review 10: rating 4.0. My old ones kept snagging the fabric & kind of unraveling it, but these made a nice clean cut with no " fabric hangys" lol pardon my lack of a word. persona: This customer prioritizes high-quality, performance-driven art supplies with superior pigment concentration, smooth application, and professional-grade results, and is willing to invest in premium products that offer durability, consistency, and creative versatility—especially those that deliver noticeable improvements over student-grade alternatives.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Unique and Beautiful Paints I’ve been working with watercolor for a year now. I’ve used several different student grade brands of watercolor, most of which are fairly  good in terms of color saturation and performance. Having tried a honey based pro brand and now Daniel Smith, I prefer the latter... and yes, pro vs student grade does make a difference with performance.  Generally, the DS colors are far more concentrated than the student grade paints, making a tiny amount go a loooong way. Also, the paper (100% cotton) absolutely loves them! That being said, the primatek colors do behave A bit differently than the regular DS paints. They granulate naturally. It’s as if the mineral base sits on top of the paper allowing for streakiness, though that may be from not using enough water in my wash. The colors are subtle, except for the rhodonite, which is a pretty vibrant pink. For beautifully subtle, granulating color, I would definitely recommend these. They do dry quicker than the regular paints. It’s as if they absorb the water differently. I also purchased the essentials set. I swatched and mixed the two sets together (photo attached), which resulted in some beautiful colors. The tubes are small, but not as tiny as other reviewers led me to believe. One of the 5ml tubes will fill a full pan with paint left in the tube. The INTERIOR of the  Meeden full pans I am using is 2.8mm L x 1.6 W x .08 H. N. When you break it down by cost, I paid less than $4 per full pan. That’s not bad for professional watercolors. The paints arrived on time and were hand delivered to me at my door. The boxes were taped closed and the tubes were full and unopened. All appropriate colors were in the boxes. I’m quite excited to do more painting with these paints!

My quilt piecing quality has really improved as well as my seams. Fabric does not creep or move when these pins are used. But, beware of handling the fabric a lot or you are sure to stick yourself. Now my seams all match & it is really a blessing to see my quilt turning out so crisp & professional, compared to my first nine patch blocks in the quilt.

To use these pins for quilting, I pressed my seams open and lined up a seam and held it in place with my fingers. At 1/4 inch from the raw edge where I would sew my seam allowance, I put in the pin so the forks go on either side of the seam with the rounded edge of the pin lining up with the raw edge. persona: This customer values precision, durability, and thoughtful design in sewing and crafting tools, consistently prioritizing high-quality, functional products that enhance efficiency and accuracy, and is willing to pay for reliable performance, even if it means occasionally encountering minor flaws or inconsistencies in specific batches.
reference_output: rating 5.0. A great invention I saw these in use on a YouTube video and HAD to get them right away to solve the problem of perfectly aligning intersecting seams.  They are so slender that you can safely machine stitch right over them, ensuring that the alignment stays just as you pinned it.  The directions on the package don't mention it, but the YouTube video said to insert the pin from left to right, i.e., toward the edge of the garment, not toward the body of the garment as one does with an ordinary straight pin, with the bent end pointing up.  They are terrifically sharp and hold tightly.  I find that prior to inserting the fork pins, accurate alignment of the intersecting seams or plaids is made certain by vertically inserting a straight pin through both layers' seams and then using the fork pin to capture the alignment securely.  Excellent quality and performance!

Will never purchase again Review 8: rating 1.0. Don't use unless you love bubbles! Disappointingly viscous resin will leave you with bubble filled castings that do not escape on their own due to the thick gooey consistency of the resin. The resin needs to be vacuum degassed or pressure cast to achieve acceptable results, and there are many resins on the market that do a better job with a much lower viscosity. I will stick with Smooth-on's Crystal Clear 202, which has given me good results so far. Review 9: rating 1.0. Awful!! I am an experienced tumbler maker and know my way around epoxy. reference_output: rating 5.0. Way better than what we paid for! I am giving these five stars simply because so many people are giving them one star because of their size! That is absolutely ridiculous and it makes me Furious! It's not the sellers fault if the buyers are too stupid to know what three mm is! I didn't know so I went and looked. All you have to do is ask Google. 3 mm is 1/8 of an inch! That's pretty tiny. These rhinestones are very sparkly and they're very tiny. It doesn't take a huge bag to put a thousand in there. I didn't count them and I'm not going to. I don't know if there's a gross of rhinestones in there. I paid $3.95 and no shipping. I'm sure I got plenty of rhinestones for my money! I'm not going to run out anytime soon. I got what I needed and I'm satisfied with the amount of money I paid, we all got a bargain yet people are complaining because they didn't know what they ordered and it became someone else's fault! So they're going to blame the sellar! That's what's wrong in this world. I'm not going to turn this into an ethical thing, but it is!! It's absolutely not being accountable for yourself and having to blame someone else for everything because you surely didn't do anything wrong! It was your money and they should have used measurements you understood, even if mm is the standard measurements for stones and jewels!

with time you may need to replace some of these items and you can upgrade accordingly. Review 3: rating 5.0. Great for a non-professional hobby. I bought this Late october the 25th or 26th and it showed up as expected. Acually, the first day of the "3-5days" which was a great plus. So, about this product.. I bought this because i'm not a huge professional artist that spends hours drawing and making a living off of it. I like to sketch, draw and cartoon. and this set allows me to do that with multiple tools and pencils. I love the variety and quality of these pencils. Including charcoal not ... Came out amazing with plenty of stickers left over to end up all over my house.  Still very happy with purchase.

Review 14: rating 5.0. Well Made, Good Selection Colors and shapes for every project. Removes from backing easily, lays flat on contact surface an adheres well.  Like that they are individually wrapped. Have put some in children's birthday cards for a little surprise.

Really patchy and hard to use You really have to layer it on for it to look ok. Be careful though, when you add a new layer, the 2.5 layers you've already put on to make it look a little less patchy will move around and wipe off easily, even when it's completely dry. It will look ok if you put work. Review 4: rating 5.0. Went on easy with just a tiny bit of water added for ... Went on easy with just a tiny bit of water added for smoothness. Nice and thick. Stayed on all night. My skin sensitive daughter had no issues using this. Review 5: rating 5.0. Did the job Was very opaque, you need a little water to get it extreme white but it will do the job. Only bad thing is it came unglued from the bottom of the packaging so have to be careful when I use it. Review 6: rating 1.0. Not impressed This did not have very good pigmentation and was hard. Review 7: rating 2.0. Wrong product Its a nice product, but I bought a mass amount of white for Addams Family Values and they all came labeled as white when the actual packaging said light grey. I can't even figure out how to contact the seller about this. It's frustrating and I need to buy more but I dont want to buy from here in fear of it being light grey again. Review 8: rating 5.0. Great value I love Snazaroo for face painting. These large single palettes of paint go a very long way. I've done at least 100 faces, mostly full, and have hardly made a dent in it yet.

Totally worth the price! Review 9: rating 2.0. It's a no from me A few people I know along with a bunch of other cosplayers who have used snazaroo said the product was great so I tried it. I used a makeup sponge to apply, getting the sponge wet like it said to and I tried dabbing it on for 20-30 with little success. I could barely cover my face and whenever I touched it it either smudged or came off completely. It even smudged in the process of applying it ?? I tried letting it dry then applying more and that only made it worse. ... Review 10: rating 1.0. Waste of money This was an absolute waste of my money and time. One Star terrible- did not go on easy, was dried up. do not recommend Review 14: rating 1.0. Old supply Received an old and dried out package. Will not buy again. Review 15: rating 1.0. reviews:
Review 1: rating 1.0. Chemical Smell These mats smelled terrible. I tried airing them out outside in the sun, washing them twice and it still was too much chemical smell. It came off on a sweater I was blocking and not sure if it will ever come out. Don't waste your money on these.

Review 2: rating 4.0. Must follow the numbered sequence in 3x3 formation for gridlines to line up Bought this to steam block a knitted lacy acrylic yarn scarf. The gridlines are definitely useful, however they only match up if you follow the numbered sequence (3x3 formation). When piecing the mats into other shapes (for example, a long rectangle for blocking my scarf) the gridlines may not line up as nicely. Be aware that the surface can get "blotchy" if the steam iron leaks. Received 100 T-pins instead o...

Review 3: rating 2.0. Strong chemical smell transferred to garment The boards make the knitted garment smell like chemicals.

Review 4: rating 5.0. Exactly what I wanted I had a chance to use these this week and they are great! they fit together perfectly, all the grid lines match up, the squares are numbered so they do match. They are seem to be nice and sturdy.  There is no smell to these mats and my set was complete with the pins and tape measure. Perfect for my crafting needs

Review 5: rating 2.0. Seems good I just got these and do like them. Can't wait to finish my current project and block it. This is my first set. However the description said: PACK OF NINE 12” BY 12” BLOCKING MATS - Includes carry bag and 100 T-pins. Portable foam blocking boards let you knit wherever you go. But mine did not come with a bag, quite disappointed in that since I really don't want to store them loose. Thus the reason why 2 stars.

Review 6: rating 3.0. You Get What You Pay For! I like it, but as other reviewers have mentioned, some of the tiles don't quite match up. My tiles also arrived dirty, with brown stains on the backs and a few of the pins were twisted & bent. Aside from these complaints, I'm otherwise satisfied (and glad I bought another separate set of pins) and recommend these as a great starter blocking set or if you need something on the fly.

Review 7: rating 2.0. New smaller mats I ordered these in Oct 2017 & again in Feb 2018.  The squares are now smaller and therefore the new mats cannot be attached to the "old" mats - an important, expected feature in order to block large shawls.  What a disappointment!

Review 8: rating 1.0. Doesn’t work with previously ordered product I bought mats from Hephaestus 3 years ago & was trying to expand the area for a larger shawl project - unfortunately these did not match AT ALL so I have returned.  Tried reordering the original product but informed it was no longer available - sad to say!!!

Review 9: rating 2.0. Only three mats actually line up. I bought two boxes of these blocking mats a while ago and am just now using them. They are packaged very nicely and the addition of a tape measure and pins is great. However, in both of my boxes, only the first three mats actually have lines that match up. I am following the 3X3 grid instructed on the box. To arrange mats 4-9 so the lines match up, I have to leave space between them after manually putting the lines in place. It's not what I expected....

Review 10: rating 3.0. Offensive Odor Quick delivery. Unfortunately this product set has a distinctly offensive odor, (the ink lines and/or foam squares) which I hope fresh air & sunshine will remove. This odor has a strong possibility of transferring into my woolen project, as the smell is very foul. Can not recommend these blocking squares. I own another set of 9 squares without this issue.

Review 11: rating 3.0. Decent product They are great mats. The lines match up pretty, unfortunately there is this bug stuck in it.

Review 12: rating 3.0. Lines only work in ONE configuration Lines do NOT line-up if you need to block in a different configuration.  Only works if you are able to use the configuration on the box.  Did arrive quickly.

Review 13: rating 1.0. Disappointing These mats were so disappointing. I had bought mats from the same company a few years ago and was very happy with them, so I “purchased again “ and they are nothing like the first ones. They are thinner and you cannot connect them to the previous ones. The notches don’t line up and the overall mat is smaller. As I said, disappointing.

Review 14: rating 1.0. Disappointed At first I thought these were great if you’re only blocking small pieces. I had 2 sets but the lace shawl I needed to block was much longer. So I bought 2 more sets. How disappointing to find none of the lines matched up to the two sets I already had. And they pulled up off the floor as I was stretching my shawl. Don’t waste your money.

Review 15: rating 1.0. Disappointing These mats were so disappointing. They are thinner and you cannot connect them to the previous ones. The notches don’t line up and the overall mat is smaller. As I said, disappointing.

Review 16: rating 2.0. Dirty product, fishy origins Now, I wasn’t expecting this product to be lifechanging... but I did expect I wouldn’t be getting rusty safety pins and paper clips in with my pins (1/10 are bent at a 90° angle to begin with). I want to block my knitting, not get tetanus.

Review 17: rating 4.0. It's wonderful, but smells terrible I worked for 35 hours on a hand knit wool blend sweater, and i soaked it in wonderful scented fabric softener, and everything was wonderful until I unpinned my sweater and it smelled like chemicals. The blocks have a very strong scent to them, and it has permeated my handknit sweater. I love the blocks, pins and tape measure, and would say buy this as long as you have time to air out the blocks and figure out a way to make them less stinky!

Review 18: rating 4.0. Meh You get what you pay for. One of my blocks was very faded. All blocks vary in quality of the printed grid on it

Review 19: rating 1.0. Smells horrible and transfers to knitting! If I could give this zero stars I would! It’s smells horrible and it transferred to my knitting. Thanks goodness it was only a gauge swatch. Don’t waste your money.

Review 20: rating 2.0. Grid doesn't line up These are fine mats, but the grid only lines up in one formation. The mats are numbered to make the 3x3 grid square easy to make but grid doesn't line up any other way making it essentially useless.

reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Wonderful, vibrant colors! I love these pens. I prefer a water based marker for my projects, mostly for painting on wood. But these pens can be used on many surfaces. The colors are vibrant and the ink comes to the tip very well! When I need more I will but the same pack! The only reason I didn’t mark the “longevity” is because I haven’t had them long enough. Review 2: rating 2.0. SmaLlllllllllllllll Way small like crayon size Review 3: rating 4.0. A few issues but overall good pens. First purchase of these Artistro pen set. I enjoy working with them however, They seem to need a lot more shaking with use and will stop putting out ink until shaken and the point pushed up and down.++111111+ The more they are used the more bits of paint, mostly dried, begins to stick. I found using a rubber glove and pulling while twisting works best. Color coverage is good. Will buy again. Review 4: rating 5.0. Perfect I used these pens to paint my wooden Catan pieces. I stained the wood, then used these pens to do the detail work. I was very happy. Review 5: rating 1.0. Terrible These are terrible you cannot prime them to get the paint to come down to the tip they are horrible to use don’t buy them do not waste your money Review 6: rating 5.0. Amazing paint pens! The paint is opaque and smooth on most surfaces I like these a LOT more than I thought I would. The color range is great, the liquid paint is very smooth and it's opaque enough to cover mistakes on my drawings. The fine tips are great for details and also feel very durable (like it can take a beating). reviews:
Review 1: rating 3.0. Shockingly low-quality item from a typically great brand. I was really, really disappointed in these... I've used Prismacolor products for years, and they've always been top of the line.

The problem I had with these was that they seemed too... dry. They require a lot of pressure, don't blend very seamlessly, and often leave scratches in the paper.

I understand that cross-spectrum blends aren't going to have a perfect gradation, but I was hoping to use these on l... Review 2: rating 5.0. Good Addition to Fabric Art Stash! Proper blending is important to the success of your project. Prismacolor colored pencils & blenders are superior products. If you are into fabric art this is a must for your art kit. Review 3: rating 2.0. Break a lot These break often and I can do a better job blending with my finger then with these pencils. Review 4: rating 1.0. TRY BLENDING YOUR PRISMAS WITH SOMETHING ELSE. Baby Oil is better than Mineral Spirits, thats for sure. brittle, hard, do NOT blend well. purchased 3 packs. NONE blend well at all Review 5: rating 5.0. I can blend and smooth like never before Whoa, this totally changes my colored pencil coloring experience! I can blend and smooth like never before! And they do last a long time! Worth it! Review 6: rating 5.0. Five Stars You can never go wrong with Prismacolor! Review 7: rating 4.0. Great I bought these to go along with the 150 prismacolor colored pencils I have. Which means I have a myriad of colors in which to blend anyways, and I do do a lot of blending with the pencils. However I love these colorless blenders because it gets rid of the little white spots when using colored pencils and makes it a more even color. Which makes it look so much better. Review 8: rating 5.0. How did I ever get by without these?! I can't believe I've never tried a blender pencil. They make ALL the difference in my coloring! They are really easy to use, and there are tons of youtube videos with examples and how-to's. They are just like a regular pencil, made with colorless wax, so when you use these to go over something you colored with multiple pencils, it will lightly blend the colors together. These are a definite must-have for me from now on. Review 9: rating 5.0. These are amazing. I use them with the Prismacolor "soft core" ... These are amazing. I use them with the Prismacolor "soft core" colored pencils. It makes the colored pencils last longer because I now press gently while adding color, so I use a smaller amount of pigment in each drawing. Then I blend with the blending pencil and the color spreads and gets darker. Now that I've started using these I can't imagine how I got along without them. Review 10: rating 5.0. Excellent Blenders Excellent pencil. My wife and I use these to blend the colors into our Lost Ocean and Enchanted Forest coloring books. I help to give the pages the nice faded looks instead of having to control how hard you have to press and you move across the page. With this we have been able to start at one point and color to shade we want then use this pencil to fade the color across to the other side to give it that pop and make it stand out and more realistic. Review 11: rating 5.0. I love them! I only buy prismacolor for these kinds of things because they work so well. I love them! Review 12: rating 2.0. Two Stars Love the blending pencils. Easy to work with Review 13: rating 5.0. blending pencils!! After learning how to blend from other fellow adult coloring enthusiast these blenders pencils were highly recommended; so I purchased them based on reviews and word of mouth; and I am very pleased with them. They do a great job of helping you blend your work after using colored pencils and they will help get rid of those pesky little white spots and make your coloring look a lot smoother and more professional. I highly recommend them!! Review 14: rating 5.0. These blenders are so much better than I expected These blenders are so much better than I expected. reference_output: rating 1.0. Waste of time and money I’ve made other ornaments, mixing my own acrylic paints with pouring medium and they are stunning-marble lined—beautiful designs and finish. Sealed those with polyurethane-no problems. After drying for 3 days, I sealed these ornaments and the dry colors began to run and left brush marks in the finish. The end result even prior to sealing was more crafty DIY looking than the professional finish I achieved mixing pouring medium, as well. I tried adding one of my self mixed colors in a layer with some of these colors and they would not mix. Literally the paint was sliding off the glass. Not sure what I can mix with these to salvage them but I will not use them again as is.

Review 1: rating 5.0. Best Entrelac Book Yet: Includes Instructions for Technique, "Designer" Rectangles, Attractive Garments Far and away the best book yet on entrelac knitting! This is the book that I wish I'd had, when I first attempted entrelac. It has big, beautiful photos that illustrate each step in the entrelac process, and it includes detailed information on things like how to avoid holes where the triangles and rectangles are joined. It also explains how to knit backward (for increased speed)... Review 2: rating 4.0. good for the basics but.... I bought this book based on the reputation of the author and, frankly, the negative reviews of the other competing book. This is a very good overview of entrelac, with basic instructions and a really great variety of patterns to play with (and some interesting projects). But it didn't cover what I had hoped for with the "entre nous" sidebars. There are issues with actually knitting entrelac -- how to handle the edges of the project when it's not seamed (sl... The detailed instruction on how to knit entrelac is worth the money. The stitchonary on different variations of entrelac blew my mind. I like this technique, and I am looking forward to re-teaching myself this technique and all the amazing variations. Every knitter should have a copy of this book. reference_output:
rating 5.0. The Building Blocks of Entrelac I have done a little entrelac in the past, but I still felt kind of confused. I've had this book forever and finally sat down with it to relearn... And I was up and running in no time.

I love the way it breaks down how basic entrelac works in a straightforward & logical fashion, like building blocks. It also has a lot of pictures that are really helpful.

One you absorb and master the basics, there are tons of projects and variations to keep you busy.

Still, if you use these to cover injection marks you won't even be able to see the difference unless you inspect the model very closely. Very roomy and enough compartments for me to house the 6 yarns I'm using to make my granny squares!!

Review 9: rating 5.0. This bag rocks, complete sanity saver!!!! I had a big storage crate full of yarn that was sitting under my dining room table forever. Half finished projects, it was a mess. The other problem I have is unless you stuff the other side with something the bag tends to fold up. It would also be nice if it had a sturdier bottom to stay put. I do however reco...

Otherwise it is well made and is great to keep the pets at bay.

Review 17: rating 5.0. Great buy! My wife’s crocheting got a little out of control so I got her this. It’s perfect! Everything fits neatly inside, it holds 6 full yarn balls in the feeding pouches, and it’s super great for traveling! Material is good quality and the bag itself seems well made.

Review 18: rating 5.0. persona: This customer is a dedicated, long-term crocheter who values high-quality, durable tools and organized storage solutions, consistently prioritizing performance and reliability over price, and demonstrates strong brand loyalty to products that enhance precision and efficiency in their craft.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Whether you knit or crochet this is the organizer to own! I know it is labeled as a "knitting" organizer, but it is equally perfect for crocheting projects! It is VERY roomy and holds a lot of yarn and all your needed tools! I especially like the clear plastic "windows" to see which colors I have, and the quantity left of a skein. The bag has multiple pockets to hold all the tools you will possibly require for any given project.  The bag is very well made, sturdy, easy to use when moving from one working location to another or if one takes it along while traveling. This is a "must have" for every knitting/crocheting enthusiast A great buy at a great price!

reference_output: rating 3.0. dull I found these pins to be dull when compared to the pins I have been using. When I looked more closely I could see that these pins have a larger diameter than my other pins and I think that explains why they don't pierce the fabric as easily. I thought perhaps I had received a bad batch but I bought more than one package of these and I found them to all be the same. These will work but I'll be using my other pins before I use these. Sure wish I could remember where I bought the other ones.
